segments.paradiso-10-g48: E is the coordinating conjunction 'and' introducing the clause; Bëatrice is the proper name of Dante's guide; cominciò is the passato remoto (3rd person singular) of cominciare, meaning 'began' and here is followed by a colon that introduces direct speech.
segments.paradiso-10-g49: Ringrazia is the 2nd person singular imperative of ringraziare ('give thanks' / 'thank'), addressed to the addressee; the comma shows the vocative or repeated command.
segments.paradiso-10-g50: ringrazia repeats the 2nd-person imperative; il is the definite article 'the'; Sol (here written without final -e) is a poetic noun 'Sun' used as a title; de is an archaic form of di = 'of'; li is the plural article 'the' (old spelling); angeli is the noun 'angels' (object of the prepositional phrase). The whole phrase is 'give thanks to the Sun of the angels.'
segments.paradiso-10-g51: ch’ is the reduced form of che 'who/that' introducing a relative clause; a (to) combines with questo sensibil = 'to this sensible (state/perception)', where sensibil is an archaic/poetic noun/adjective used substantively; t’ is the clitic for te (you) placed before the auxiliary; ha levato is the compound verb (ha = auxiliary 'has' + levato = past participle 'raised'), meaning 'has raised'; per sua grazia is a prepositional phrase 'by his/its grace'.
segments.paradiso-10-g52: Cor di mortal = 'heart of a mortal' (poetic word order); non fu mai is the negative + passato remoto 'was never'; sì digesto here uses digesto in an older sense 'so disposed/apt' (not the legal 'Digest'); a divozione e a rendersi a Dio = 'to devotion and to rendering/surrendering oneself to God' where rendersi is pronominal 'to render oneself/submit oneself'; con tutto ’l suo gradir cotanto presto = 'with all its delight so quickly', cotanto modifying presto.
segments.paradiso-10-g53: come = 'as' introducing the comparison; a quelle parole = 'at those words' (a + definite phrase); mi fec’ io is an inverted, contracted form meaning roughly 'I made myself / I was made (so)' — fec' is the archaic contracted form of fece (make/do) with the clitic mi 'me', and the final io emphasizes the subject.
segments.paradiso-10-g54: e sì tutto ’l mio amore in lui si mise = 'and so all my love put itself in him'; si mise is a reflexive/passive-se construction with mettere in the passato remoto mise 'put', meaning 'was placed' or 'settled' into 'him' (lui) — i.e., all the speaker's love transferred to him.
segments.paradiso-10-g55: che = 'so that' / 'that' introducing the result clause; Bëatrice is the proper name subject; eclissò is passato remoto of eclissare 'eclipsed/overshadowed'; ne l’oblio = ne (in/into) + l’oblio 'the oblivion' meaning 'into oblivion.'
segments.paradiso-10-g56: Non le dispiacque; = 'It did not displease her.' Dispiacere is used impersonally like English 'to displease' or Spanish 'disgustar' — the thing (implicit) displeased (or did not) the indirect object le ('to her'). dispiacque is passato remoto (third-person singular) of dispiacere. Non negates the whole clause. Note that le here is an indirect-object pronoun, not the feminine article.
segments.paradiso-10-g57: ma sì se ne rise, = 'but she so laughed about it.' ma = 'but.' sì is an intensifier ('so/so much'). se is an archaic/poetic spelling/variant for the reflexive si here (marks the reflexive construction of 'ridere' when used with ne), and ne = 'of it/about it' (partitive/relational pronoun). rise is the passato remoto (3rd-person singular) of ridere in an archaic form (modern Italian would be 'rise' → 'rise' in the text; functionally 'she laughed'). The clause therefore gives a brief result/response: she did not take offense but laughed instead.
segments.paradiso-10-g58: che lo splendor de li occhi suoi ridenti mia mente unita in più cose divise. = 'that the radiance of her smiling eyes divided my mind, united into many separate things.' che introduces the result clause. lo splendor = 'the radiance' (lo as article before s- or other consonant); de li is the archaic form of modern dei ('of the'); occhi suoi ridenti = 'her smiling eyes' (ridenti is a present participle/adjective 'smiling' placed after occhi and suo is the possessive). mia mente unita = 'my mind, united' (unita is past participle used adjectivally). in più cose divise = 'into many divided/separate things' (divise = past participle/adjective). The line is compact: the brightness in her eyes causes the speaker's previously united mind to be split into many distinct thoughts.
segments.paradiso-10-g59: Io vidi is passato remoto (vidi) meaning “I saw/witnessed.” più folgór vivi e vincenti is a descriptive noun phrase: più here = 'several/more' functioning as an intensifier; folgór (poetic for folgore) = 'flashes/lightning'; vivi and vincenti are coordinatied adjectives modifying folgór. Far ... far is a duplicated infinitive construction from fare: far di noi centro e di sé far corona means ‘make of us a center and make of themselves a crown’ (di noi = object/target, di sé = reflexive possessor). Word order places the verbs (infinitives) after the descriptive phrase for rhythmic effect.
segments.paradiso-10-g60: più ... che is a comparative construction: 'more ... than ...'. Here più dolci in voce che in vista lucenti compares two qualities — 'sweeter in voice than (they are) shining in appearance.' In voce means 'to the ear' (by sound), in vista 'to the sight' (by appearance); lucenti is an adjective agreeing with the omitted subject (the flashes).
segments.paradiso-10-g61: così = 'thus/so.' cinger is the archaic infinitive form of cingere ('to gird, encircle') used for metrical reasons. La figlia di Latona = 'the daughter of Latona' (mythological reference). vedem talvolta is an archaic or poetic first-person-plural present ('vedem' = vediamo) meaning 'we see sometimes'; the infinitive cinger and the finite vedem form a loose comparison: 'thus do we sometimes see (her) girded.'
segments.paradiso-10-g62: quando introduces a temporal clause 'when.' l’ aere è pregno uses present indicative (è) with pregno in the adjectival sense 'full/charged' (of vapor, moisture, or humidity). The clause gives the circumstance in which the visual effect is seen: 'when the air is thick/laden.'
segments.paradiso-10-g63: sì che introduces a result clause 'so that/with the result that.' ritenga is present-subjunctive (from ritenere) here used to express the resulting containment or restraining power of the air; it could be read as 'so that it holds back / may hold back.' Il fil = 'the thread' (fil = thread); che fa la zona is a relative clause 'which forms/makes the zone/belt' (fa = makes). Zona here denotes a 'belt' or ring (the halo around the moon).
segments.paradiso-10-g1: Guardando is a present gerund (literally 'looking') used here to introduce the action accompanying the main clause. "nel suo Figlio" is a prepositional phrase (in + definite article contracted + possessive + noun) meaning 'in/into his Son'. "con l’Amore" ('with the Love') indicates the instrument or accompanying force. The relative "che" begins a clause describing that Love; "l’uno e l’altro" ('the one and the other') is a paired nominal phrase joined by e, and "etternalmente spira" uses the present indicative of spira (from spirare, 'breathe/emanate') in a literary register to mean 'eternally breathes forth'.
segments.paradiso-10-g2: "lo primo e ineffabile Valore" is a nominative noun phrase (the first and ineffable 'Valore' — here best understood as 'Power/Valor'). "quanto per mente e per loco si gira" is a clause introduced by quanto ('how' / 'insofar as'), where "per mente e per loco" means 'with regard to mind (intellect) and to place' and "si gira" is a middle/reflexive present ('turns itself', 'revolves'). "con tant’ ordine fé" ends the clause with con + noun 'with such order' and fé, an archaic past tense of fare used poetically to mean 'made' or 'fashioned'.
segments.paradiso-10-g3: Ch’ is the contracted form of che introducing a result or explanatory clause ('that'/'so that'). "esser non puote" uses the infinitive esser with an archaic form puote (from potere) meaning 'can' — together 'cannot be'. "sanza gustar di lui" is a negative adverbial phrase ('without tasting/enjoying/experiencing of him'), where sanza is an older form of senza. "chi ciò rimira" is a relative clause 'whoever contemplates/looks upon this', rimira being a literary verb 'to contemplate'.
segments.paradiso-10-g4: Leva is an imperative (second-person singular) meaning 'raise' or 'lift' in an older/poetic register; dunque is an adverb 'then/therefore' marking the sequence or consequence.
segments.paradiso-10-g5: Lettore is the direct address vocative 'reader' and is set off by the comma; no grammatical change in the clause caused by it.
segments.paradiso-10-g6: A + definite article + noun (a l’alte rote) means 'to the high/lofty wheels' — here a poetic reference to the celestial spheres. "meco" is an enclitic prepositional compound meaning 'with me', and "la vista" is the direct object 'the sight/gaze'; word order places the prepositional phrase before the object for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-10-g7: Dritto a quella parte is a directional phrase 'straight to that part'. "dove" introduces a relative clause 'where', and "l’un moto e l’altro si percuote" uses two nouns joined by e ('one motion and the other') with the reflexive si on the verb percuote ('strike/beat against'), meaning they strike one another.
segments.paradiso-10-g8: E lì comincia a vagheggiar is 'and there he begins to contemplate/admire' — comincia + a + infinitive is the standard periphrastic 'begins to ...'. Ne l’arte 'in the art' marks the domain of contemplation; "di quel maestro che dentro a sé l’ama" is a relative clause 'of that master who loves him within himself', where dentro a sé is 'within himself' (reflexive).
segments.paradiso-10-g9: Tanto che introduces a result clause 'so that'. The clause "mai da lei l’occhio non parte" is a negative construction where mai + non yields 'never'; da lei means 'from her/it' (referring back to the feminine antecedent), and l’occhio non parte is 'the eye does not depart/leave' (a poetic way of saying the eye never turns away).
segments.paradiso-10-g10: Vedi is the imperative or present indicative 'see/behold'. Come introduces a comparative/explanatory clause ('how/as'). Da indi is an adverbial phrase 'from there/thence' (indi = thence). Si dirama is a reflexive verb 'branches off/spreads out', and l’oblico cerchio is the subject 'the oblique/slanting circle'. The relative che introduces 'that carries the planets' (che + porta).
segments.paradiso-10-g11: Per sodisfare is an infinitival phrase 'in order to satisfy/so as to satisfy' (per + infinitive used purposefully). "al mondo" ('to the world') is the indirect object or beneficiary, and "che li chiama" is a relative clause 'which calls/summons them' where li refers back to the planets.
segments.paradiso-10-g12: Che se introduces a conditional clause ('for if'). "la strada lor" is the possessed noun phrase 'their road/path' (lor = their). "non fosse torta" uses the imperfect subjunctive fosse (from essere) plus the adjective torta ('crooked'/'bent') to mean 'were not bent' — a counterfactual condition.
segments.paradiso-10-g13: Molta virtù nel ciel sarebbe in vano: molta (much) modifies virtù; here virtù is best read as 'power/effectiveness' of the heavens. Nel ciel is locative 'in heaven', and sarebbe is conditional 'would be', with in vano 'in vain'.
segments.paradiso-10-g14: E quasi ogne potenza qua giù morta; is a rhetorical continuation: quasi 'as if', ogne potenza 'every power', qua giù 'here below', and morta is an adjective 'dead' used figuratively; the clause expresses that the celestial powers would be as if dead.
segments.paradiso-10-g15: E se dal dritto più o men lontano fosse ’l partire: another conditional 'and if the departure from the straight were more or less distant'. Dal dritto 'from the straight', più o men 'more or less', and fosse 'were' with partire used substantively (the departure).
segments.paradiso-10-g16: Assai sarebbe manco states the result: assai 'much' / 'very', sarebbe 'would be', manco 'less' or 'diminished'. "e giù e sù de l’ordine mondano" means 'both down and up of the worldly order' — i.e. the hierarchical order of the world would be much diminished; the phrases giù and sù are adverbial/figurative positions.
segments.paradiso-10-g17: Or is an adverb meaning “now.” Riman is an archaic imperative (second-person singular) of rimanere (modern rimani) meaning “remain” or “stay,” and the enclitic ti is the clitic object/pronoun (“you/yourself”) attached to the verb. The whole unit addresses the reader with a command to stay put.
segments.paradiso-10-g18: Lettor is the vocative form of lettore (“reader”), used to directly address the audience; the comma shows it is a direct address rather than part of the verb phrase.
segments.paradiso-10-g19: Sovra ’l tuo banco is a prepositional phrase giving the place of the staying: sovra (poetic form of sopra) = “on/above,” ’l = il (the) elided before vowel, tuo = “your,” banco = “bench/seat.” Together: “on your bench/seat.”
segments.paradiso-10-g20: Dietro pensando is an adverbial gerund phrase: dietro used adverbially = “in looking back / in retrospect,” and pensando is the gerund of pensare meaning “thinking.” A introduces the object of thought (“about”), ciò che = “that which,” and si preliba is a reflexive/impersonal present (si + preliba) meaning “is foretasted / is tasted in advance” (prelibare = to foretaste). The clause modifies how the reader should sit: while thinking back on what has been foretasted.
segments.paradiso-10-g21: S’esser vuoi is a contracted poetic sequence for se esser vuoi = “if you want to be.” Here s’ is an archaic/contracted form of se (if). Esser is the infinitive essere. Vuoi is the second-person singular present of volere (“you want”); the construction means “if you want to be.” Lieto assai = “very glad” (lieto + assai). Prima che stanco = a temporal subordinate (“before [you are] tired”), where che introduces the subordinate clause and stanco is a predicative adjective. (Note: the printed s’ in the word-list is a contraction of se; its listed senses do not include the conjunction “if.”)
segments.paradiso-10-g22: Messo is the past participle of mettere used here with the auxiliary ho to form a perfect: Messo t’ho innanzi = “I have placed you before (me).” The clitic t’ is the direct object (“you”) attached before the auxiliary ho; innanzi = “in front / before.” Word order is poetic—past participle fronted for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-10-g23: Omai is an adverb meaning “now/by now/henceforth.” Per te = “for your sake” or “on your behalf.” Ti ciba: ciba is a present form (here best read as a jussive/subjunctive/hortatory third person: “may it feed/sustain you”), with the clitic ti the object (“you”). The implied subject of ciba is the thing previously put forward (what will “feed” the reader).
segments.paradiso-10-g24: Ché = “for/because.” A sé torce tutta la mia cura quella materia ... is a clause with inverted word order: torce (present of torcere) = “turns” or “bends” and a sé = “to itself” (reflexive adverb). Tutta la mia cura = “all my care.” Quella materia is the grammatical subject placed after the verb for emphasis: “that subject.” Ond’ io son fatto scriba = relative clause “from which I am made a scribe”; ond’ = onde/whence. Son fatto is a passive/periphrastic perfect (“I am made / I am rendered [a scribe]”) meaning his being a scribe comes from that subject.
segments.paradiso-10-g25: Lo ministro maggior de la natura is a noun phrase: Lo = the article, ministro = “minister/agent,” maggior = “greater/chief” used attributively, de la natura = “of Nature.” The phrase personifies some cosmic agent (“the chief minister of Nature”).
segments.paradiso-10-g26: Che introduces a relative clause modifying ministro. Del valor del ciel = “of the power/virtue of heaven.” Lo mondo imprenta: imprenta (present) = “imprints/presses” and lo mondo = “the world” (archaic article + noun) is the object; the verb is placed after its object for poetic order. E col suo lume il tempo ne misura = “and with his light measures out time for it/us,” where ne is a clitic referring back to the effected thing (“for it” or “of it”), and misura = “measures.”
segments.paradiso-10-g27: Con quella parte che sù si rammenta congiunto = “joined with that part which is recalled above.” Con = “with,” quella parte = “that part,” che sù si rammenta = relative clause where sù = “above” and si rammenta is reflexive/passive “is remembered/recalled.” Congiunto is a past participle/adjective meaning “joined.” The sequence shows the minister is joined to the previously mentioned 'part.'
segments.paradiso-10-g28: Si girava is the imperfect reflexive form (“was turning/was revolving”), indicating continuous action. Per le spire = “through the coils/spirals” (movement through a spiral path). In che più tosto ognora s’appresenta is a relative clause: in che = “in which,” più tosto = “more quickly/sooner,” ognora = “ever/always,” and s’appresenta (si appresenta) = “presents itself / appears.” The whole describes a revolving motion in which something appears ever more quickly.
segments.paradiso-10-g29: E io era con lui is straightforward: e = “and,” io era = imperfect “I was,” con lui = “with him.” This places the poet alongside that turning agent.
segments.paradiso-10-g30: Ma del salire non m’accors’ io = “but I did not notice the ascent.” Ma = “but.” Del salire = “of the ascent” (a noun phrase). Non + accors’ io is an archaic perfect of accorgersi (“I did not notice / I was not made aware”), with the clitic m’ = mi (“to/for me”) attached; the printed lemma/sense list for the clitic and accors in the supplied word-list do not match their actual function here, so the appropriate sense indices are unavailable.
segments.paradiso-10-g31: Se non com’ uom s’accorge = “except as a man notices.” Se non here introduces an exception/comparison (“except that / save that”), com’ = com’ (short for come) = “as/like,” uom = “man/person,” s’accorge = reflexive present of accorgere = “he notices/realizes.” The clause sets up the manner in which the poet failed to perceive the ascent (only as a man would perceive it). (Note: the supplied senses for se were not the conjunction ‘if/except’ and so are marked below as absent.)
segments.paradiso-10-g32: Anzi ’l primo pensier = “even before the first thought” or “indeed the first thought.” Anzi here has the sense of temporal precedence (“even before / indeed”), l = the article, primo pensier = “first thought.” It prepares the final temporal phrase that follows.
segments.paradiso-10-g33: Del suo venire = “of his coming/arrival.” Del = di + il (“of the”), suo = “his,” venire used as a noun = “coming/arrival”; it completes the clause: the poet did not notice the ascent except as a man notices (even) before his first thought of the arrival.
segments.paradiso-10-g34: È Bëatrice quella che sì scorge di bene in meglio, = literally 'It is Beatrice, the one who so perceives from good into better.' È is the 3rd-person singular present of essere ('is'). quella che is a demonstrative + restrictive relative clause ('the one who'); sì is an adverb intensifying scorge (3rd-person singular present of scorgere, 'discerns/sees'); di bene in meglio is a concise idiomatic phrase meaning 'from good into better' (di = 'from', bene = 'good' used as substantive, in meglio = 'into better'). Word order is emphatic/poetic but grammatically straightforward: subject (È Bëatrice) + identifying phrase (quella che ...) + verb phrase.
segments.paradiso-10-g35: sì subitamente che l’atto suo per tempo non si sporge. = 'so/so suddenly that her act does not project itself in time.' sì subitamente is an adverbial intensifier ('so/so suddenly' or 'so swiftly'); che introduces a result clause. l’atto suo is 'her act' (atto = act/gesture; suo = her/its). per tempo here means 'in/within time' or 'in time.' non ... si sporge is a negated present 3rd-person singular of sporgere with reflexive si ('does not project/extend/manifest itself'); the reflexive si marks that the action affects itself (i.e. the act does not present itself within the proper time). The phrasing is somewhat poetic/elliptical.
segments.paradiso-10-g36: Quant' is a contraction of quanto ("how much"); esser is the infinitive 'to be' placed before the finite verb convenia (3rd-person imperfect of convenire, "it was fitting"), so the inverted order expresses "how much it was fitting to be...". Da sé is a prepositional phrase meaning "by itself" (da = by, sé = itself); the noun phrase quel ch'era dentro al sol is a relative clause "that which was inside the sun", and dov' io entra'mi is a relative‑adverbial clause with an archaic past form entra'mi (past tense with the enclitic mi), meaning "where I entered" and attaching to the preceding antecedent.
segments.paradiso-10-g37: This short clause is elliptical: non per color = "not by color." Non negates the following prepositional phrase (per + color); per here means "by/through (means of)" and the clause denies that the appearance was due to visible color.
segments.paradiso-10-g38: Ma introduces the contrast ("but/rather"); per lume attaches the cause or manner "by/through light," and parvente is a present participle/adjective meaning "appearing" or "seeming." The whole phrase says the phenomenon presented itself as light rather than as color.
segments.paradiso-10-g39: Perch' is an abbreviated perché ("because"); the subject pronoun io is emphatic. Lo is the direct object clitic "it" placed before the coordinated nouns 'ngegno (archaic spelling of ingegno, "intellect/talent"), l'arte and l'uso ("art" and "usage/practice"). Chiami is present indicative, first person singular "I call/name."
segments.paradiso-10-g40: Sì nol direi is inversion of non lo direi ("I would not say it"); direi is conditional present ("I would say"). Che introduces the subordinate clause; mai means "ever" in the negative sense and s'imaginasse is the imperfect subjunctive (3rd person) with the reflexive/clitic s' used impersonally — "that anyone could ever have imagined."
segments.paradiso-10-g41: Ma introduces a counterpoint ("but"); creder puossi is an impersonal construction (infinitive creer + the verb form puossi) meaning "it can be believed/one can believe" — puossi is an older impersonal form of poter. Di veder is an infinitive phrase "to see" placed before the verb for emphasis, and si brami uses the impersonal clitic si with brami to mean "one longs/desires," so the phrase says it is believable and one desires to see it.
segments.paradiso-10-g42: E se begins a conditional clause ("and if"); le fantasie nostre places the possessive after the noun (common in Italian) and son is the contracted present of essere (sono = "are"). Basse is the plural adjective "low," and a tanta altezza is a prepositional phrase "to/for so great a height," indicating the standard to which the imaginations are insufficient.
segments.paradiso-10-g43: Non è maraviglia is a fixed impersonal expression meaning "it is not a wonder" or "no marvel." Non negates the copula è and maraviglia is the noun "wonder/marvel."
segments.paradiso-10-g44: Ché = "for/because." Sopra 'l sol is a locative phrase "above the sun" (sopra + the elided article l); non fu is the perfect/past form "there was not" (fu = was), occhio is the subject "eye," and ch'andasse is a relative clause with andasse in the imperfect subjunctive (used after a negative antecedent) meaning "that had gone" or "that had reached" — i.e. no eye had gone above the sun.
segments.paradiso-10-g45: Tal = "such"; era is the imperfect/past "was" and quivi is an adverb "there." La quarta famiglia is the noun phrase "the fourth family," and de l'alto Padre uses the older de (di) + article to mean "of the High Father." Word order (Tal era quivi ...) is the normal poetic inversion for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-10-g46: Che introduces a relative clause "which"; sempre is the adverb "always/ever;" la is the feminine clitic object pronoun placed before the verb (referring to the family) and sazia is the present indicative 3rd-person singular of saziare "satiates/fulfills," so the clause means "which always satisfies it/them."
segments.paradiso-10-g47: Mostrando is a gerund "showing/revealing" that links to the previous clause; come is used twice as a comparative/introducer meaning "how/as," spira is a present-tense verb form ("breathes/emanates") in the sense of breathing forth, and figlia is a noun "daughter/offspring." The phrase describes the way the family both "breathes" (proceeds) and is a daughter (a produced one).
segments.paradiso-10-g95: L’altro is the nominative noun phrase ('the other') with the elided article L’ (= il). ch’ is the relative pronoun (ch’ = che) introducing the relative clause. Appresso is an adverb meaning 'next' or 'nearby' and precedes the verb in poetic word order; addorna is 3rd-person singular present indicative 'adorns' with il nostro coro as its direct object.
segments.paradiso-10-g96: Quel Pietro fu: quel + proper name is a demonstrative identifying the person, and fu is the passato remoto (he/it was). che introduces another relative clause. con la poverella marks accompaniment ('with the poor little woman'), offerse is an archaic past form (offerso) meaning 'offered', and a Santa Chiesa suo tesoro places the indirect object ('to Holy Church') and the possessed noun ('his treasure').
segments.paradiso-10-g97: A simple noun phrase serving as subject: La quinta luce = 'the fifth light', with the feminine ordinal quinta agreeing with luce.
segments.paradiso-10-g98: ch’ (= che) introduces a relative clause 'which is among us most beautiful'. È is present indicative 'is'. tra noi is a locative phrase 'among us', più bella is a comparative/superlative description 'more/most beautiful' modifying luce.
segments.paradiso-10-g99: spira is 3rd-person singular present of spirare used poetically as 'breathes out' or 'emits'; di tale amor is the complement 'of such love' that explains what it breathes.
segments.paradiso-10-g100: che introduces a result/consequence clause: 'such that'. Tutto ’l mondo là giù is the subject phrase 'all the world down there'. Ne gola is an idiomatic construction where ne refers back ('of it/about it') and gola (here verbal/figurative) expresses eager longing; di saper novella uses the infinitive saper with novella as its object, 'to know news'.
segments.paradiso-10-g101: entro v’è is a locative inversion ('within there is'); l’alta mente is the noun phrase 'the lofty/high mind' that is in that place. u’ = ove/wherein 'in which', sì profondo modifies saver, and fu messo is the compound passive in passato remoto ('was placed'), with saver (abstract 'knowledge') as the direct object.
segments.paradiso-10-g102: che here is the conjunction resuming and linking to the following explanatory clause ('that,'). It signals the upcoming conditional/commentary.
segments.paradiso-10-g103: se is the conditional 'if'. 'l vero è vero' is a tautological locution 'if truth is truth' (emphatic); è is present indicative 'is'.
segments.paradiso-10-g104: a veder tanto is an idiom meaning 'to see so much' (a + infinitive used to indicate the act of seeing/experiencing). non surse il secondo: surse is passato remoto of sursire 'to rise up', so 'the second did not rise' — i.e. no second could compare or 'no second rose up' to see that amount.
segments.paradiso-10-g105: Appresso vedi = 'next you see' (appresso = next/afterwards; vedi is 2nd-person singular present used to point something out). Il lume di quel cero = 'the light of that candle' (possessive/of-phrase). che introduces a relative clause: giù in carne = 'down there in the flesh' (meaning during earthly life), più a dentro is an adverbial phrase 'more inwardly/deeper', and vide is passato remoto 'saw' with l’angelica natura e ’l ministero as the compound direct object.
segments.paradiso-10-g106: Ne l’altra piccioletta luce = 'In the other little/tiny light' (ne = in/of it here marking location), ride = 'smiles/laughs' (3rd-person singular present). Quello avvocato de’ tempi cristiani = 'that advocate of Christian times' (demonstrative + noun phrase). del cui latino Augustin si provide is a relative clause: del cui = 'of whom/whose', latino specifies 'in Latin' or 'the Latin [version]', Augustin is the subject, and si provide is reflexive 'provided for himself/saw to' or 'provided' (here meaning Augustine supplied or made provision in Latin).
segments.paradiso-10-g86: The speaker uses the past tense self-identification: 'Io fui' is the passato remoto ('I was'). 'de li agni de la santa greggia' is an archaic/poetic expression meaning 'of the lambs of the holy flock' (de + definite article li = modern degli). The relative clause 'che Domenico mena per cammino' uses 'che' = 'who/which' and the present verb 'mena' ('leads'), giving the characteristic or habitual action of Domenic; 'u’' (= ove) 'wherein', followed by the impersonal/reflexive 's’impingua' ('one is enriched', with enclitic 's’' = 'si') and the negative conditional clause 'se non si vaneggia' ('if one does not rave/think fancifully').
segments.paradiso-10-g87: 'Questi' is a demonstrative 'this one'; 'che' is the relative 'who/which'. The phrase 'm’è a destra più vicino' literally places him 'to me on the right, nearer': 'm’' = 'mi' (to me) appears as an enclitic before the verb 'è' (here 'è' functions as 'is located'), and 'più vicino' is a comparative 'closer/nearest'.
segments.paradiso-10-g88: The nominal phrase 'frate e maestro' ('brother and teacher') is followed by the archaic fused verbal form 'fummi'. Here 'fummi' must be read as 'fu' + 'mi' (he was to me) — an older enclitic combination meaning 'he was (to) me', so the sense is 'he was my brother and teacher.'
segments.paradiso-10-g89: 'ed esso Alberto è di Cologna' : 'ed' = 'and'; 'esso Alberto' is an emphatic reference 'that same Albert'; 'è' = 'is' and 'di Cologna' gives origin ('from Cologne') using 'di' for provenance.
segments.paradiso-10-g90: 'e io Thomas d’Aquino.' is a simple nominative apposition: 'and I [am] Thomas of Aquino.' The verb 'essere' is omitted (understood) after the conjunction; 'd’' is the elided form of 'di' indicating origin/family.
segments.paradiso-10-g91: This conditional starts with 'Se' ('if') and 'sì' ('so'/'thus'), followed by the prepositional phrase 'di tutti li altri' ('concerning all the others'). The infinitive 'esser' ('to be') precedes the archaic contracted present 'vuo’' (modern 'vuoi', 'you want'), and 'certo' is the predicative adjective 'certain' — overall: 'If you thus want to be certain of all the others.'
segments.paradiso-10-g92: The phrase 'di retro al mio parlar' means 'behind (i.e. following) my speech/words'. The particle 'ten' is archaic/elliptical (here best rendered 'then' or a hortative particle) and is followed by 'vien' (come/come along) — a present/hortative verb form inviting the addressee to 'come' or 'follow' — 'col viso girando su' = 'with your face turning upward' and 'per lo beato serto' = 'over the blessed wreath.'
segments.paradiso-10-g93: 'Quell’ altro fiammeggiare' = 'that other flashing/blaze' (note the elision Quell’ = quel + elision). 'esce' is the present of 'uscire' ('issues/comes forth') and 'del riso di Grazïan' means 'from the laughter/smile of Gratian' (Grazïan = the canonist Gratian).
segments.paradiso-10-g94: The relative clause 'che l’uno e l’altro foro aiutò' uses 'che' = 'who' and the coordinated noun 'l’uno e l’altro foro' = 'both the one and the other forum/court' (i.e. both legal forums). 'aiutò' is passato remoto 'he helped', followed by result phrase 'sì che' (= 'so that') + 'piace in paradiso' ('he is pleasing/has favor in Paradise'), meaning his help rendered him pleasing in Paradise.
segments.paradiso-10-g115: Questi (demonstrative pronoun) refers back to what was just said and functions as the source/topic ('from these things') of the following statement; onde is an adverbial/relative 'whence' or 'from which' (here meaning 'from these'), a is the preposition 'to/for', me is the indirect object 'to me', ritorna is present indicative 3rd singular 'returns', and il tuo riguardo is the noun phrase 'your regard/attention' as the grammatical subject that 'returns' toward the speaker.
segments.paradiso-10-g116: The clause begins with è (present 'is') introducing a predicative identification: 'it is the light of a spirit'; d’uno spirto ('of a spirit') is a genitive noun phrase; the relative che introduces a subordinate clause describing that spirit: 'who, in/under thoughts (che ’n pensieri gravi a morir), seemed to him (li parve) to come (venir) late (tardo)'. Note li parve uses the archaic indirect-object pronoun li = 'to him' and parve is the perfective past-form 'seemed' (parve = parveva/pareva in usage), while venir tardo is an infinitive phrase functioning with parve.
segments.paradiso-10-g117: essa (emphatic pronoun 'it/that') resumes the preceding idea and is equated by è to la luce etterna ('the eternal light') with di Sigieri indicating possession/association ('of Sigieri'). The word order esse + definite noun phrase is a standard equative structure.
segments.paradiso-10-g118: che is a simple relative pronoun introducing the following explanatory relative clause (’who/which’). It links 'la luce etterna di Sigieri' to the action that follows.
segments.paradiso-10-g119: leggendo is a gerund ('reading' / 'while reading') that opens a circumstantial participial clause; nel (in + il) marks location 'in the', Vico is treated here as a title or proper name in which Sigieri was reading (so it's a locative/title), de li is the old form of 'dei'/'delle' meaning 'of the', and Strami is a noun ('Strami' = 'straw' or the name of the work/place). The whole phrase means 'reading in the Vico de' Strami'.
segments.paradiso-10-g120: silogizzò is a past-tense (past historic) 3rd-person singular meaning 'he syllogized' or 'he reasoned/deduced'; the direct object invidïosi veri ('spiteful/envious truths') is a plural noun phrase in which invidïosi is an adjective modifying veri. The clause reports an intellectual act attributed to Sigieri.
segments.paradiso-10-g121: Indi is an adverb meaning 'then' or 'thence', used to sequence actions; it stands alone as a temporal connector at the start of the sentence.
segments.paradiso-10-g122: This whole simile begins with come ('like/as') and compares the scene to 'an orologio' (clock) governed by a relative clause: che ne chiami ne l’ora ('that calls us there at the hour') where chiami is a 3rd-person form used in a relative/comparative sense (here subjunctive coloring is possible in literary Italian), ne indicates 'there/in it', and ne l'ora = 'at that hour'. The subordinate infinitive phrase a mattinar lo sposo ('to rouse the bridegroom') expresses the clock's purpose, and perché + ami introduces a final/purpose clause with ami as a subjunctive 'so that she may love him' (l' = 'him', object pronoun clitic attached to the verb).
segments.paradiso-10-g123: che introduces a further descriptive clause: l’una parte e l’altra ('one part and the other') is a coordinated nominative phrase, and tira e urge are two present indicative verbs ('pulls and urges/presses') both applying to those 'parts' — a compact description of two forces acting together.
segments.paradiso-10-g124: tin tin is onomatopoeic repetition representing the bell-sound; sonando is the gerund 'sounding/ringing' describing how the clock acts, con introduces the instrumental phrase 'with so sweet a note' where sì dolce nota is an emphatic adjectival phrase modifying nota.
segments.paradiso-10-g125: che resumes and gives the effect of that sound: 'that the well-disposed spirit of Love swells.' l' (the) + ben disposto (adverbial/adjectival phrase 'well-disposed' or 'well-inclined') modifies spirto (spirit); d'amor is a genitive 'of Love', and turge is a present form 'swells' (3rd-singular), describing the spiritual reaction.
segments.paradiso-10-g126: così ('thus') links back to the simile and introduces the main perception: vid' ïo (colloquial truncated past 'I saw') places the speaker as witness. la gloriosa rota is the direct object 'the glorious wheel'; muoversi is the infinitive with reflexive ending indicating 'to move' (here reported as what the wheel does), e render voce a voce ('and give voice, voice by voice') uses render (render/give) plus repetition of voce to indicate successive voices; in tempra e in dolcezza ('in temper and in sweetness') are adverbial phrases describing manner. The relative ch'esser non pò nota se non colà dove gioir s'insempra qualifies that sweetness as something that cannot be named except 'there where joy is ever renewed' — note archaic forms (vid' for vidi, s'insempra reflexive) and that pò and several clitics are medieval spellings with functions equivalent to modern poter/si.
segments.paradiso-10-g107: Or is an adverb meaning "now." Se tu introduces a conditional clause ("if you ..."). l'occhio de la mente is literally "the eye of the mind," where l' is the elided definite article and de is an archaic variant of di ("of"); the phrase means the inner sight or mental gaze. Trani is an archaic/poetic verb-form (here meaning something like "you draw/guide/turn") addressed to tu; di luce in luce is a repeated prepositional phrase "from light into light" showing motion of the mental gaze, and dietro a le mie lode means "following my praises," where dietro a = "after/following" and le mie lode (literally "my praises") is the object of dietro a.
segments.paradiso-10-g108: Già is an adverb meaning "already." de l' ottava is an archaic way to say "of the eighth" (l'ottava = the eighth sphere/level); con sete literally "with thirst" describes the state you will be left in. Rimani is the second-person form meaning "you remain" (present form used here as the result in the apodosis of the conditional). Note the older prepositional form de and the compact word order where the verb rimani closes the clause.
segments.paradiso-10-g109: Per + infinitive (Per vedere) marks purpose or intent: "to see/behold." Ogne is an archaic spelling of ogni ("every"), and ben here is a noun meaning "good(s)" or "goodness." Dentro vi places that good "within it/there," with vi functioning as a locative adverb "there/in it." Gode is the present tense of godere and means "rejoices/derives delight," and l'anima santa is the grammatical subject that follows the verb (inverted order for poetic effect).
segments.paradiso-10-g110: Lo corpo = "the body." Ond' is an elided form of onde/ond ("from which/whence"), introducing a relative clause: "the body from which she was expelled." Ella fu cacciata is a passive perfect (fu + past participle) meaning "she was expelled." Giace is present "lies" and giuso "down/below" gives the location; in Cieldauro is the place name ("Cieldauro").
segments.paradiso-10-g111: Ed = "and." Essa refers back to the soul (subject). Da martiro e da essilio uses da with an ablative/instrumental sense "by/through martyrdom and by exile." Venne is the past tense venire "came" and a questa pace means "to this peace/rest" (venne a = "came to"). The clause says the soul attained this peace through martyrdom and exile.
segments.paradiso-10-g112: Vedi is the second-person singular present of vedere ("see"/"behold") used as an exhortation "look/see." Oltre = "beyond/further." Fiammeggiar is an infinitive (used after vidi/vedi to introduce what one is to behold) meaning "to flame/blaze." L' ardente spiro is the direct object where l' is the article and ardente describes spiro as "ardent/burning." Spiro here is an archaic/formal noun meaning "breath" or "spirit" (the supplied sense list gave verb senses, so the noun sense is irregular/archaic). d'Isidoro is the genitive "of Isidore."
segments.paradiso-10-g113: This short segment is a simple list: di Beda e di Riccardo = "of Bede and of Richard," with di marking the genitive/association to the preceding "ardent spirit."
segments.paradiso-10-g114: Che introduces a relative clause referring to the nearest antecedent (Richard). A + infinitive (a considerar) is an idiomatic construction meaning roughly "in being considered" or "when/if one considers"; considerar = "to consider/regard." Fu is the past tense "was." Più che = "more than," so più che viro says he was "more than a man," with viro an archaic nominative form for "uomo."
segments.paradiso-10-g64: Ne here is the poetic preposition meaning "in" (not the modern clitic 'ne'); the phrase orders article + noun normally: 'Ne la corte del cielo' = 'In the court of Heaven.' 'del' is the contracted preposition + article 'di + il.'
segments.paradiso-10-g65: ond' (ondè/onde) is a relative/adverb meaning 'whence' or 'from which'; io is the 1st-person pronoun; rivegno is an older/present-tense form meaning 'I return' (Dante often uses present or historic-present forms for narration).
segments.paradiso-10-g66: Si trovan is an impersonal/reflexive construction meaning 'are found' (literally 'one finds/they find themselves'); molte gioie care e belle is the substantive phrase 'many dear and beautiful joys.' Tanto che introduces a result clause 'so that' followed by non si posson trar 'they cannot be drawn' — trar is the apocopated infinitive (trarre). 'del regno' = 'out of the realm/kingdom.'
segments.paradiso-10-g67: E 'l canto di quei lumi era di quelle: e = 'and'; 'l (the) canto' = 'the song'; 'di quei lumi' = 'of those lights' (lumi = lights/illuminations, i.e. souls/angelic lights); 'era di quelle' links back to le 'gioie' (quelle) — 'the song of those lights concerned/was of those [joys].' Era is imperfect 'was.'
segments.paradiso-10-g68: Chi non s'impenna sì che là sù voli: 'chi' = 'whoever'; non + s'impenna = 'does not rear/raise himself' (impennare used metaphorically: to lift oneself up or rear up); sì che introduces the result 'so that' and voli here expresses the result 'flies' (in form used by Dante). The clause says: whoever does not raise himself so as to fly up there....
segments.paradiso-10-g69: Dal muto aspetti quindi le novelle: 'dal muto' = 'from the mute' (muto = silent one); aspetti here functions as an exhortation/expectation 'you should expect' or 'expect' addressed to the reader/listener; quindi = 'then/therefore'; le novelle = 'the tidings/news.'
segments.paradiso-10-g70: Poi = 'then' or 'afterwards'; a simple temporal adverb starting the next action in the narrative.
segments.paradiso-10-g71: Sì cantando is a gerundial phrase 'singing thus' (sì = 'so/thus', cantando = gerund 'singing'), describing how the movement occurs.
segments.paradiso-10-g72: Quelli ardenti soli si fuor girati intorno a noi tre volte: quello = 'those'; ardenti = present participle 'burning' (descriptive); soli here functions as 'suns' in context (Dante often calls angelic lights 'soli'); si + fuor (archaic variant of fuori) + girati = reflexive perfect 'they turned themselves out/around' (fuor = 'out' in a poetic form); intorno a noi tre volte = 'around us three times.'
segments.paradiso-10-g73: Come stelle vicine a' fermi poli is a simile: 'like stars close to the fixed poles' — come = 'like'; stelle = 'stars'; vicine = 'near'; a' (a + elision) = 'to/at'; fermi poli = 'fixed poles' (the idea is of stars that hardly move, close to the axis).
segments.paradiso-10-g74: Donne mi parver: 'donne' = 'women'; mi = 'to me'; parver is an archaic past form of parere (compare modern 'parvero'/'parvero') meaning 'they seemed.' The construction means 'they appeared to me as women.'
segments.paradiso-10-g75: Non da ballo sciolte: 'non' negates; 'da ballo' = 'for dancing' (da + noun expresses purpose or relation), so 'not loosened/loose for dancing' — 'sciolte' (past participle/adjective) describes them as not 'loose for a ball/dance', i.e. not capriciously dancing about.
segments.paradiso-10-g76: Ma che s'arrestin tacite: 'ma' = 'but'; 'che' introduces a relative clause; s' (reflexive) + arrestin (present subjunctive/indicative form) = 'that they stop/cease' (here 'arrestin' describes their halting); tacite = 'silent', so 'but who halt silently.'
segments.paradiso-10-g77: Ascoltando fin che le nove note hanno ricolte: ascoltando (gerund) = 'listening'; fin che = 'until'; le nove note = 'the nine notes' (musical notes); hanno ricolte = 'have gathered/been re-collected' — Dante uses ricolte to mean the completion or re-collecting of the musical phrase, so 'listening until the nine notes are complete.'
segments.paradiso-10-g78: E dentro a l'un senti' cominciar: 'E' = 'and'; 'dentro a l'un' = 'within one (of them)'; senti' is the contracted/poetic past form of 'sentii' ('I heard'); cominciar is the infinitive 'to begin' — the narrator reports hearing someone begin to speak.
segments.paradiso-10-g79: Quando lo raggio de la grazia: 'Quando' = 'when'; 'lo raggio' = 'the ray' (metaphorical radiance); 'de la grazia' = 'of grace' (grazia = divine favor). This begins the quoted conditional/temporal clause explaining how love is kindled.
segments.paradiso-10-g80: Onde s'accende verace amore e che poi cresce amando: 'onde' = 'from which/whereby'; s'accende is the pronominal verb 'si accende' meaning 'is kindled' or 'becomes aflame' (the reflexive/pronominal form shows passive or intransitive causation); verace amore = 'true love'; 'e che poi cresce amando' = 'and which afterwards grows by loving' (poi = afterwards, cresce = grows, amando = by loving).
segments.paradiso-10-g81: Multiplicato in te tanto resplende: 'multiplicato' (past participle) = 'multiplied' or 'made manifold'; 'in te' = 'in you'; 'tanto resplende' = 'shines so much/so brightly' (resplende is present 'shines' — Dante's diction often uses forms that may look subjunctive, but here it's descriptive).
segments.paradiso-10-g82: Che ti conduce su per quella scala u' sanza risalir nessun discende; 'che' = 'that'; 'ti conduce su' = 'leads you up'; 'per quella scala' = 'by/along that ladder' (scala = ladder/ladder of ascent); 'u'' = archaic 'where'; 'sanza risalir' = 'without re-ascending' (sanza = without; risalir = to climb up again); 'nessun discende' = 'no one descends' — the clause describes a ladder where descent never happens except after an ascent (complex Dantean usage).
segments.paradiso-10-g83: Qual ti negasse il vin de la sua fiala per la tua sete: 'qual' = 'he who' / 'who'; 'ti negasse' (subjunctive/conditional) = 'should deny you' or 'would deny you'; 'il vin de la sua fiala' = 'the wine from his phial' (vial = poetic container); 'per la tua sete' = 'because of/for your thirst' (i.e., because you are thirsty).
segments.paradiso-10-g84: In libertà non fora se non com' acqua ch'al mar non si cala: 'in libertà non fora' uses archaic conditional 'fora' (Dante's equivalent of 'would be'/'would exist') — 'would not be in liberty/freedom'; 'se non com'' = 'except as' or 'but as'; 'acqua ch'al mar non si cala' = 'water that does not fall into the sea' (ch' = che = 'that'; cala = 'falls/goes down'). The sentence concludes the hypothetical: such a person would not be free except as water that does not reach the sea.
segments.paradiso-10-g85: Tu vuo' saper = 'You want to know' (vuo' is a contracted poetic form of vuoi, followed by the infinitive saper 'to know'). 'Di quai piante s'infiora questa ghirlanda' = 'of which plants this garland is flowered/embroidered' — s'infiora is a pronominal/passive construction meaning 'is adorned with flowers' (si marks the intransitive/pronominal form). 'Che 'ntorno vagheggia la bella donna' = 'which wanders/is displayed around the fair lady' (che = which; 'ntorno = intorno = around; vagheggia here carries the sense 'is borne around/plays about' in a poetic sense). 'Ch'al ciel t'avvalora' = 'who in heaven supports/strengthens you' (al = a + il; avvalora = 'gives power/support').

sentences.paradiso-10-s13: And Beatrice began: "Give thanks, give thanks to the Sun of the angels, who by his grace has raised you to this sensible state."
sentences.paradiso-10-s14: No mortal heart was ever so ready for devotion and for yielding itself to God, with all its delight, so quickly as I was at those words; and so all my love was set in him that Beatrice was eclipsed into oblivion.
sentences.paradiso-10-s15: It did not displease her; but she laughed about it so, that the radiance of her smiling eyes divided my once-united mind into many separate thoughts.
sentences.paradiso-10-s16: I saw several vivid, triumphant flashes of light make us their center and make of themselves a crown, sweeter in voice than bright to sight: thus we sometimes see the daughter of Latona girded, when the air is heavy, so that it holds back the thread which forms the belt.
sentences.paradiso-10-s1: Looking into His Son with the Love that both eternally breathe, the first and ineffable Power — insofar as it turns in intellect and in place — He made with such order that whoever contemplates this cannot be without tasting of Him.
sentences.paradiso-10-s2: So lift then, reader, your gaze with me to the high wheels, straight to that part where one motion and the other strike each other; and there begin to contemplate, in the art of that master who loves him within himself, so much that the eye never parts from it.
sentences.paradiso-10-s3: See how from there the oblique circle branches off that carries the planets, to satisfy the world that calls them.
sentences.paradiso-10-s4: For if their path were not bent, much power in heaven would be in vain, and as if every potency here below were dead; and if the departure from the straight were more or less far, there would be far less up and down in the worldly order.
sentences.paradiso-10-s5: Now remain, reader, upon your bench, thinking back on that which one foretastes, if you would be very glad before you are tired.
sentences.paradiso-10-s6: I have set you before me: now, for your sake, may it feed you; for my whole care turns to that subject of which I am made a scribe.
sentences.paradiso-10-s7: The greater minister of Nature, who imprints the world with the power of heaven and with his light measures time for it, joined with that part which is recalled above, was turning through the coils in which it ever more quickly presents itself; and I was with him; but I did not notice the ascent, except as a man notices, even before his first thought, his coming.
sentences.paradiso-10-s8: That is Beatrice, who perceives from good to better so quickly that her action does not make itself apparent in time.
sentences.paradiso-10-s9: How bright by itself that which was within the sun — where I entered — ought to have been; not by color, but seeming as light!
sentences.paradiso-10-s10: Because I call it intellect and art and habit, I would not say that anyone ever imagined it; but it can be believed, and one longs to see it.
sentences.paradiso-10-s11: And if our imaginations are low for so great a height, it is no wonder; for above the sun there was no eye that had gone.
sentences.paradiso-10-s12: Such was there the fourth family of the High Father, which ever satisfies it, showing how it breathes and how (it is) a daughter.
sentences.paradiso-10-s25: The other, who next adorns our choir, was that Peter who, together with the poor little woman, offered his treasure to the Holy Church.
sentences.paradiso-10-s26: The fifth light, which among us is most beautiful, breathes such love that all the world down below yearns to know news of it: within it is the lofty mind in which knowledge was placed so profoundly that, if truth is truth, the second did not rise to see so much.
sentences.paradiso-10-s27: Next you see the light of that candle which, down in the flesh, saw the angelic nature and its ministry more inwardly.
sentences.paradiso-10-s28: In the other tiny light smiles that advocate of Christian times, of whom Latin Augustine provided.
sentences.paradiso-10-s21: I was one of the lambs of the holy flock that Dominic leads along the way where one is enriched if one does not rave.
sentences.paradiso-10-s22: This one, who is nearest to my right, was my brother and my teacher; that Albert is from Cologne, and I am Thomas of Aquino.
sentences.paradiso-10-s23: If you wish to be certain about all the others, come behind my speech with your face turned upward over the blessed garland.
sentences.paradiso-10-s24: That other flashing issues from the laughter of Gratian, who helped both courts so much that he is pleasing in Paradise.
sentences.paradiso-10-s33: It is from these things that your gaze returns to me; it is the light of a spirit who, being weighed down by thoughts of dying, seemed to him to come late: that is the eternal light of Sigieri, who, reading in the Vico de' Strami, syllogized spiteful truths.
sentences.paradiso-10-s34: Then, like a clock that summons us at the hour when the Bride of God rises to rouse the bridegroom so that she may love him — which one side and the other both pull and press, ringing tin-tin with so sweet a note that the well-disposed spirit of Love swells — thus I saw the glorious wheel move and give voice, voice to voice, in temper and in sweetness that cannot be named except there where joy is ever renewed.
sentences.paradiso-10-s29: Now if you guide the eye of your mind from light to light, following my praises, you will already remain at the eighth with thirst.
sentences.paradiso-10-s30: To behold every good within it, the holy soul rejoices there; she makes the deceptive world manifest to whoever hears well of her.
sentences.paradiso-10-s31: The body from which she was expelled lies down below in Cieldauro; and she, by martyrdom and by exile, came to this peace.
sentences.paradiso-10-s32: Look farther and see the ardent breath (spirit) of Isidore blazing, and of Bede and of Richard; he (Richard), when considered, was more than a man.
sentences.paradiso-10-s17: In the court of Heaven, whence I return, many dear and beautiful joys are found — so many that they cannot be drawn out of the realm; and the song of those lights was about them; whoever does not lift himself up so as to fly up there should, therefore, expect the tidings from the mute.
sentences.paradiso-10-s18: Then, singing thus, those burning suns turned themselves around us three times, like stars near the fixed poles; they seemed to me like women, not loosened for dancing but who stand still, silent, listening until the nine notes have been gathered.
sentences.paradiso-10-s19: And within one of them I heard begin: 'When the ray of grace — from which true love is kindled and which afterwards grows by loving —, multiplied in you shines so brightly that it leads you up that ladder where no one descends without ascending again; whoever denied you the wine of his phial because of your thirst, would not be in liberty except like water that does not fall into the sea.'
sentences.paradiso-10-s20: You want to know of which plants this garland is flowered — this garland that wanders around the fair lady who supports you in heaven.
